title: heliophila seselifolia subsp. nigellifolia
intro: Heliophila seselifolia subsp. nigellifolia, commonly known as the hairy heliophila, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the Western Cape region of South Africa, where it grows as a small, annual plant with hairy leaves and stems. Traditionally, the hairy heliophila has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are typically made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water.
